response: The Latin name for one of the five "big cats" that the word "Panther" may refer to when outside of the Americas is **Panthera pardus**. This is the scientific name for the leopard, which is one of the five big cats in the genus *Panthera*. The leopard is also referred to as "Panther" in various regions outside of the Americas, such as in South America, where it is known as the Jaguar (*Panthera onca*).
